title: Add blog post
description:
Please use the attached audio, photo, and document for "Olga S." and post the blog to the Hill & Moin website.
Relevant links
https://hillmoin.com/latest-news/


 

Title/Headline:

"Woman Injured In Defective Sidewalk Trip And Fall Accident Awarded $448,000"

Body:

"Life has a way of unfolding quietly—until, without warning, everything changes. On a peaceful autumn day, Olga S. stepped out of her home and began walking alone, enjoying the brisk seasonal air. It began as an ordinary moment, but as she moved along the sidewalk, her foot suddenly caught on a raised edge in the concrete. In an instant, Olga lost her balance and fell hard to the ground, the shock of the impact matched only by the pain that followed.
Disoriented and injured, Olga quickly realized that this was more than a simple misstep. The fall left her with injuries that disrupted her daily life and forced her to confront the reality that this accident was caused by dangerous and negligent sidewalk conditions. Determined to seek accountability and fair compensation, Olga reached out to Hill & Moin, LLP—an excellent firm known for handling trip and fall cases with skill, care, and determination.From the very beginning, Olga found Hill & Moin to be professional, attentive, and reassuring. Their steady, consistent communication gave her confidence throughout every stage of the case, and she was grateful that it always felt like the team was truly listening to her questions and concerns from start to finish. The attorneys at Hill & Moin guided her through the legal process with clarity and compassion, making a stressful experience far more manageable.
Thanks to the hard work and dedication of Hill & Moin, Olga’s case concluded with a strong and positive result, settling for an impressive $448,000. With her case resolved, Olga plans to use her settlement funds for the upkeep and renovations of her home—an investment in comfort, stability, and her peace of mind. Looking back, she is deeply appreciative of the support she received and thankful to have had Hill & Moin by her side every step of the way."
 
__________________

Thanks!

title: Anonymous Client Blog Post -- Hill & Moin, LLP
description:
Please post the blog below as it relates to one of their clients.

*They wanted to remain completely anonymous. Please also use a stock photo if you can that will relate to this blog post.

 
In the summer of 2019, a Brooklyn woman was involved in a serious accident. Her case presented complex legal challenges, particularly around the circumstances of the collision—issues that are often left for a jury to decide at trial. Despite these hurdles, Hill & Moin LLP took swift and strategic action on her behalf.


 Through thorough investigation and skilled legal advocacy, Hill & Moin secured a court order establishing the defendant’s liability well before the case ever reached a jury. This rare pre-trial determination of fault, in a case where each party claimed the other ran a red light, gave the plaintiff a significant advantage and eliminated much of the uncertainty that typically accompanies litigation in cases of this nature.


 Thanks to the firm's early success, the case was settled for nearly the full amount of the $1,000,000 policy limit. Hill & Moin’s reputation for tenacity, legal skill, and personalized care—reflected in their numerous five-star client reviews—was once again demonstrated in this result, allowing their client to receive critical compensation without the added burden of a lengthy trial. 


Thanks much!

description:
Seeking help post the blog below from the client.

URL: https://hillmoin.com/

Why You Should Choose a Women-Owned Personal Injury Law Firm—and Why Hill & Moin LLP Is the Right Choice

"When choosing a personal injury law firm, expertise and compassion are equally essential. That’s why selecting a women-owned personal injury law firm can be a powerful decision. Women-led firms often bring a unique, client-centered approach that prioritizes listening, empathy, and fierce advocacy—qualities crucial when you’re navigating the emotional and physical toll of an injury.

Hill & Moin LLP, a women-owned and operated personal injury law firm based in New York, exemplifies these values. With decades of experience and a track record of winning millions in settlements and verdicts for their clients, the attorneys at Hill & Moin combine legal excellence with genuine care. Clients often say it feels like having “a lawyer in the family”—because the team makes you feel seen, heard, and protected.

What sets Hill & Moin apart is their unwavering dedication to justice and integrity. They don’t treat clients like case numbers—they treat them like people. With a bilingual team and a highly personalized approach, they make sure every client stays informed, confident, and supported throughout their legal journey.

Choosing a women-owned firm like Hill & Moin isn’t just about supporting diversity—it’s about choosing a legal team that will fight for you with both skill and heart. If you’ve been injured and need strong, compassionate representation, Hill & Moin LLP is the right choice. With them, your case isn’t just business—it’s personal."

title: Add blog under elevator cases
description:
Seeking help with the request below:

Request: please post the blog from the client under their Elevator cases.

Header: Hill & Moin LLP Secures Life-Changing Settlement for Injured Apprentice During Pandemic 

Title: Over $5 Million Recovered for Injured Elevator Trainee: A Hill & Moin Success Story

Body:
At Hill & Moin LLP, our mission has always been to stand up for the people who are too often overlooked — and that mission was never clearer than in our recent victory on behalf of a young elevator trainee who suffered a catastrophic injury on the job.

During the height of the COVID-19 pandemic, our client — a newly hired apprentice in the elevator industry — was working at a job site when he fell from a ladder due to unsafe conditions. In an instant, a promising career and a young man’s physical future were jeopardized. He had been eager to learn, willing to work, and was showing up to a dangerous site in the middle of a global crisis — all in pursuit of a better life.

Instead, he faced surgeries, chronic pain, and mounting medical bills.

Hill & Moin LLP immediately took on the case, knowing that this client didn’t just need a lawyer — he needed advocates. Our legal team conducted a thorough investigation, bringing in top-tier experts in workplace safety, vocational rehabilitation, and orthopedic injury. Despite the pandemic disrupting court schedules, insurance timelines, and even medical access, we kept the case moving forward with urgency and precision.

We fought back against efforts to minimize his injuries and discount his future earning potential. We refused to let him be treated as expendable or “too inexperienced” to matter. Through aggressive negotiation and ironclad case development, we secured a settlement exceeding $5 million — a result that will allow our client to live with dignity and receive the care he needs for the rest of his life.

This outcome wasn’t just about money. It was about recognition — that an apprentice matters just as much as a foreman. That safety is a right, not a privilege. That when someone gets hurt because of negligence, we will hold the responsible parties accountable — no matter how large or well-defended they are.

What set this case apart was not only the result, but the way we approached it. At Hill & Moin, we take a personal interest in our clients’ recovery — not just legally, but emotionally. We knew our client was scared, in pain, and unsure of his future. We made sure he never had to wonder what came next.

This case is proof that you don’t have to go it alone. Whether you’re a seasoned professional or just starting out, we treat every client like family — because that’s what they deserve.

If you or someone you know has suffered a serious workplace injury, especially as an apprentice or trainee, contact Hill & Moin LLP today. Let us help you build a safer, more secure future.

Thanks much!

title: "E.C." -- Blog Post Only
description:
Seeking help to add blog post on the site. Blog post only.

 
Headline: "Courier Worker Struck By Vehicle Awarded Six-Figure Settlement"

Body:  
"During his shift as a delivery driver, E.C was getting through his route like any other day. However, this day would not go as planned. During one of his stops, E.C. was parked in a lot and had unloaded the order. As he cautiously made his way to the rear entrance of the building a vehicle suddenly and without warning reversed into him. He was blindsided and thrown violently to the ground from the impact. As the shock of what had occurred began to wear off, E.C. knew that he needed to come up with a plan. He headed to the hospital  to get checked out and discovered that he had sustained various soft tissue injuries to both sides of his body. Feeling like he deserved better, E.C. reached out to the esteemed attorneys at Hill & Moin, LLP to assess the unfortunate situation. He found comfort in the steadfast dedication Attorney Eric Wittels in navigating towards the six-figure settlement E.C. received. Facing forward to his future, E.C. intends to start a savings fund for any other unforeseen situations that fate throws his way."

title: "Alina Ordonez" -- Blog Post and Photo
description:
Seeking help to add blog post on the site. Blog post and image only.

Headline: “Home Health Aide Injured On The Job Obtains Settlement Award of $700,000”

Body:  
"After a long day of work caring for patients as a home health aide, the only thing Alina Ordonez had on her mind was relaxation. Unfortunately for her on one ill-fated evening, the very opposite occurred. Heading out into the early evening after finishing caring for a patient, Alina made her way out of the doorway and across the dark, unlit pathway. Before she knew it, she tripped and fell on the hazardous premises head-first into the nearby gate. As she found herself on the ground and in pain, she knew she needed immediate assistance. Her first call was to her son who called an ambulance, and she was rushed to the nearest hospital to treat her multiple soft tissue injuries. Knowing that her fight was just beginning, Ms. Ordonez consulted with the attorneys at Hill & Moin, LLP and she was pleased that she did. Describing Attorney David Zwerin as “courteous and hard-working”, she always felt taken care of by the communicative and responsive staff at Hill & Moin. Alina was smart to place her trust in their expertise as the attorneys successfully negotiated an impressive $700,000 settlement on her case. Family being her priority, Alina Ordonez now looks forward to investing her funds in projects that will grow and continue to provide for her and her loved ones for years to come."

title: "M.C." -- Blog Post Only...
assignees: Andrei Pocol, Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Jordan, Predrag, Venus Saragena
description:
Please add the blog post below. Blog post only. Thanks much.

Headline: “Over $600,000 In Compensation Secured For Family of 9/11 Victim”
 
Body:
The tragic events of September 11th, 2001 devasted the nation, especially New Yorkers. But for M.C. and her family, it will forever be felt even closer to the heart. M.C.’s husband had been employed as a worker for the sanitation department in Manhattan. Despite the emotional turmoil of the situation, he pressed on and continued with his duties on the job. What he may not have been aware of was that he was left exposed to various nasty toxins and chemicals, day in and day out for many hours. Fatefully, this eventually led to the development of his fatal lung cancer. M.C. and their family were crushed by their loss and reached out to Hill & Moin, LLP for guidance and support in the wake of their tragedy. Recognizing that the attorneys had significant experience navigating 9/11 Victim’s Compensation claims, she was certain that they would be able to prevail. Although the circumstances which brought M.C. to Hill & Moin’s door were heart-rending, she was comforted by the way they handled her claim and the delicate care that they showed, all while laboriously securing her a settlement of over $600,000 for the devastation she and her family had experienced.  Beginning to build back her life, M.C. hopes to invest her newly awarded funds in home improvement projects to brighten up her days.

title: "Will Reyes" -- Blog Post and Photo
description:
We got a support ticket from the client to add the blog post below. They only have an image and no audio. Thanks much.

Headline: “Victim of T-Bone Crash Awarded Settlement For Multiple Extremity Injuries”

Body:
Driving in traffic can be frustrating and stressful but depending on the situation, it can also make the ride especially dangerous. On one unsuspecting autumn afternoon, Will Reyes unfortunately found this to be his reality. Driving straight with the right of way on the highway, Will hardly had time to react to the vehicle which had startlingly began to enter the lane of traffic in which he was traveling. In the blink of an eye, the careless vehicle had pulled out into Mr. Reyes’ path, striking his vehicle on the side. Will’s body was jolted and pushed into the interior frame and steering wheel. His car came to a rest in a grassy median and it was then that he realized he was hurt. He was swiftly seen by medical staff and found to have sustained injuries to his shoulder, leg, and knee. Wishing to set things right, he called Hill & Moin to talk more about what he had been through. Upon reviewing the facts, they formulated a plan and got straight to work on his case. Through diligence and patience, Hill & Moin was able to secure Will a settlement of $167,000.  Looking back, Mr. Reyes has only good things to remember about his experience with Hill & Moin, noting that he gives them “all 5 out of 5 stars”. Looking forward, he has plans to resume his career and continue forward and to buy a fresh set of wheels for his new life on the go.

title: "S.B." -- Blog Post
description:
We got a support ticket from the client to add the blog post below. There is no audio and image. 

Headline: “Woman Involved In Motor Vehicle Accident While Celebrating Sister’s Birthday Awarded $200,000”

Body:  
When one pictures life’s joyous occasions, such as celebrating a birthday, laughter and happiness are what you might hope or even expect to experience. Woefully, S.B. wasn’t so fortunate. Setting off for an event to celebrate her sister’s birthday, S.B. and several pals climbed into the back seat of the car being driven to the venue by another friend. As they traveled along the roadway, S.B. and her friends were chatting quietly amongst themselves. Without warning, the car began to slow and just split second later, a second out of control vehicle ploughed into S. B’s host vehicle from behind. Attempting to brace herself, S.B. was batted around the inside of the vehicle like a human pinball due to the force of the impact. Although she tried to push through the distressing situation to enjoy her sister’s celebration, S.B. couldn’t ignore the growing pain she found herself to be in. Seeking medical attention, it was confirmed that she had suffered damage to her knee, shoulder, neck, and back and would require corrective surgery to treat her soft tissue injuries. Worried about what to do next and looking for clarity, on a trusted friend’s recommendation S.B. went straight to Hill & Moin, LLP. Knowing that the firm had decades worth of knowledge and expertise, S.B. knew that the esteemed counsel of Hill & Moin would be able to confidently pursue her case and find her the relief and compensation she sought. Reflecting on her relationship with the firm, she described Hill & Moin as “great!”. An admirer of their straightforward and reliable communication, she found herself at ease with how the questions and concerns she found herself asking were invariably met with confident answers. With strategy and persistence, the attorneys at Hill & Moin negotiated a $200,000 award for all that S.B had pushed through. With the new promise of a fresh start, S.B. now looks forward to investing in properties while also aspiring to open a hair salon of her very own.

Antonia and her husband were delighted by the news and immensely grateful for the work Hill &

their dream of purchasing their first home together a reality.

  • Answer: If your personal injury lawyer has retired, closed their office, or is no longer practicing, don’t panic—but do act promptly. You have the right to choose a new attorney, and it’s important to do so as soon as possible to avoid missed deadlines, court appearances, or settlement opportunities. Start by requesting your complete case file and confirming any upcoming court dates or statute-of-limitations issues so your matter remains protected during the transition.

    You should contact a well-established and skilled personal injury firm such as Hill & Moin LLP, 212-668-6000, which can immediately review your file, explain where your case stands, and seamlessly take over representation. An experienced firm will coordinate the transfer of records, communicate with insurance carriers and opposing counsel, and make sure your pending case does not go off track—allowing you to focus on your recovery while your legal rights remain fully safeguarded.

title: Homepage update
description:
We analyze the homepage and there is a list of updates that we need to implement to improve the performance. The plan is to make updates without changing the design.

1. We need to bring the H1 at the top, this is an image to show the changes, which are to transform text to image, add text paragraph, and move up the H1 element.

Text to add, align center under H1:

Our New York Personal Injury Attorneys are dedicated legal professionals who represent individuals injured due to negligence, helping them recover compensation for medical bills, lost wages, and pain and suffering. Backed by the trusted experience of Hill & Moin, we focus on personalized support, guiding clients through every step of the legal process while fighting to secure the best possible outcome.

Alternative text for image 1 and image 2 (there are two image conversions in the screenshot below): Personal injury lawyer in NYC / Personal injury lawyer in New York




2. Add informational info boxes that show up when you hover the mouse over the testimonial videos or/and their titles. I am not 100% how the functionality should be, maybe an icon with the information sign "i" added on the right side of the number $1250000 and when you move the mouse over it the info box appears, or no icon and if you hover the mouse anywhere on the title, or on the number, the info box appears. I think the icon with the "i" sign will look good, maybe this is the right way, let me know what you think.

Text 1: After suffering a workplace fall, Pedro Santos struggled to find legal representation until Hill & Moin took on his case and fought through a challenging defense process. Their dedication secured a successful outcome that provided financial stability for his family and long-term peace of mind.

Text 2: After a serious car accident in Brooklyn left her with significant injuries, the client turned to Hill & Moin for support during a difficult time balancing family, work, and medical care. Their patient, compassionate approach led to a successful outcome that exceeded expectations, bringing her relief and a sense of justice.



3. This section takes a lot of scrolling space on mobile, is it possible to shorten the length of these boxes by half for all of them so they fit in one screen?



4. This section  also takes a lot of scrolling space on mobile, can we make the icons smaller to fit two in a row?



In this section we also need to add info boxes when the mouse cursor is over one of the profiles, below are the texts for each lawyer:

Melisande Hill short bio text: With more than 40 years of experience, Melisande Hill has built a reputation for securing multimillion-dollar verdicts and fighting aggressively for injured clients across New York. Known for her compassionate approach and creative trial strategies, she treats every client like family while pursuing the strongest possible outcome for their case.

Cheryl Eisberg Moin short bio text: With more than 40 years of experience, Cheryl Eisberg Moin has successfully handled complex personal injury and appellate cases, securing multimillion-dollar verdicts and landmark decisions for injured clients across New York. Known for her sharp litigation skills and compassionate client approach, she fights tirelessly to achieve justice while making clients feel supported every step of the way.

David Zwerin short bio text: As a partner and senior trial attorney at Hill & Moin, David Zwerin is known for his relentless work ethic and dedication to helping injured New Yorkers secure justice after serious accidents. With extensive courtroom experience and a client-first approach, he fights aggressively to maximize compensation while guiding clients through every stage of the legal process.

Eric Wittels short bio text: Eric Wittels is a dedicated trial attorney at Hill & Moin known for combining aggressive courtroom advocacy with personalized client support. Drawing on his background in insurance defense, he fights to maximize compensation for injury victims while guiding them through every step of the legal process with clarity and compassion.

5. Can we add a hidden tag to describe the video? if possible, this is the text:

Hill & Moin treats every client like family, combining compassionate support with aggressive advocacy to secure life-changing results. Their dedicated team works closely with clients through every step, delivering strong settlements that help rebuild lives and restore hope after serious injuries.




Ket me know if you have any questions or are hard to implement, thanks.

title: Reporting Call Prep
description:
This task must be fully updated by EOD Monday before the reporting call.

This document serves as:
  • Internal alignment between AM, CM, and SEO
  • Source of truth for call structure
  • Risk detection tool
  • Talking point preparation
No one should walk into the reporting call unprepared.

This prep aligns directly with the live call agenda.

AM should use this document to guide the call in real time.

No one should enter the call without this fully filled out.

SECTION 1: OPENING FRAME (AM)


What tone are we setting?

Client temperature this month:
 ☐ Strong
 ☐ Neutral
 ☐ Slightly concerned
 ☐ Frustrated

Notes from weekly pulse:


Any relationship items to acknowledge at opening?

Opening focus reminder:
What do we want the client to feel by minute 3?

SECTION 2: PERFORMANCE OVERVIEW (CM SECTION)


@LeadSEO please provide by Wednesday 12 PM:
  1. How is performance this month?
  2. Campaign health status: Green / Yellow / Red
  3. Areas of concern
  4. Anything stand out? Wins or things we need to be aware of?
  5. Do you need anything from the client?
  6. What is our main focus next month?
AM must review and confirm alignment with CM before call.

Risk Flag:
 ☐ No performance concerns
 ☐ Minor fluctuation
 ☐ Underperformance risk

If risk exists:
  • Has CM been aligned?
  • Has AM Lead been notified?
No surprises allowed on call.

SECTION 3: PERFORMANCE Q&A PREP


List anticipated performance questions:




Confirm:

☐ CM prepared for these
☐ Messaging aligned
☐ Time boundary plan set

SECTION 4: WORK COMPLETED (AM SECTION)


This mirrors the “Campaign Execution” portion of the call.

List in client-friendly language:
  • SEO deliverables completed:

  • Content published:

  • Links built:

  • Technical fixes:

  • Dev completed:

  • Wins (Sentinel, visibility gains, authority growth):

No internal jargon.

SECTION 5: WORK IN PROGRESS (AM)


  • Active SEO initiatives:

  • Content pipeline:

  • Technical initiatives:

  • Dev in progress:

  • Strategic pushes:

Must clearly show momentum.

SECTION 6: TEAM NEEDS (AM)


Do we need anything from the client?
  • Approvals:

  • Content:

  • Access:

  • Decisions:


If nothing needed, state clearly: None at this time.

SECTION 7: MONTH AHEAD VISION (AM)


This section drives confidence.

Answer clearly:
  • What is our main focus next month?
  • What are we monitoring closely?
  • What would success look like by next reporting call?

SECTION 8: FINAL Q&A PREP (AM)


Non-performance questions the client may raise:


Plan response or follow-up pathway.

Confirm:

☐ If unsure, defer and follow up
☐ CM will not re-enter unless pre-discussed

SECTION 9: ESCALATION CHECK


Before call, confirm:

☐ SEO updates received
☐ Dev updates confirmed
☐ Any blockers escalated
☐ AM Lead notified if risk exists
☐ CM aligned on any sensitive topics

If any box cannot be checked, escalate before call.

SECTION 10: CALL CONTROL REMINDERS (AM)


  • Set agenda at beginning
  • Protect time at minute 20
  • Transition clearly
  • Do not guess
  • Close confidently

description:
POST #1

When a routine errand turned into a painful fall on icy Brooklyn sidewalks, S. Batool wasn’t sure what to do next. After slipping on snow-covered ice and injuring her right hand and wrist, she reached out to the Law Office of Hill & Moin for help.

Our team explained her options clearly, stayed in close communication, and provided support every step of the way. After diligent advocacy, her case settled for $500,000.

We’re proud to have helped S. Batool get the justice she deserved, and we’re wishing her the very best as she looks ahead to a new chapter with her family.

Learn More:
https://hillmoin.com/latest-news/an-icy-fall-in-brooklyn-and-a-500000-outcome/



--------------------

POST #2

A quiet autumn walk turned into a painful ordeal for Olga S. when she tripped on a defective, raised sidewalk and suffered serious injuries. What seemed like an ordinary day quickly became overwhelming as the accident disrupted her daily life and raised concerns about her recovery and future.

Olga turned to Hill & Moin, LLP for help, seeking accountability for the dangerous sidewalk conditions. From the start, our team provided clear guidance, consistent communication, and compassionate support throughout every step of the legal process.

Thanks to diligent advocacy, Olga’s trip-and-fall case settled for $448,000. She now plans to invest the funds into home renovations and upkeep, bringing comfort and peace of mind.

If you’ve been injured due to unsafe property conditions, we’re here to help you understand your options.


Learn More:
https://hillmoin.com/latest-news/woman-injured-in-defective-sidewalk-trip-and-fall-accident-awarded-448000/

title: Page design
description:
hi I prepared this page https://hillmoin.com/faqs/, can you finish the design? It should look like normal service page with. header, you can use that image, and also add a footer, so nothing special just to make it look right. And this is the schema for it:

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "@id": "https://hillmoin.com/faq/#faqpage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I contact an attorney after I get injured?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes. Speaking with an attorney early helps protect your rights, preserve key evidence, and avoid mistakes when dealing with insurance companies or employers."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I sue my employer after a work accident?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Usually no, because workers’ compensation generally prevents lawsuits against your direct employer. However, you may be able to pursue a third-party claim against a property owner, contractor, subcontractor, or equipment manufacturer."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I recover compensation if I was partially at fault?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes. New York follows comparative negligence, which means you can still recover compensation even if you were partially responsible. Your recovery may be reduced by your percentage of fault."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What should I do immediately after a construction accident?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Get medical care immediately, report the incident, document the scene if possible, and avoid giving recorded statements to insurers before speaking with an attorney."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is New York Labor Law 240?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Labor Law 240 (the “Scaffold Law”) protects workers injured by gravity-related hazards, such as falls from heights or injuries caused by falling objects, and can impose liability on owners and contractors who fail to provide proper safety protections."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is New York Labor Law 241(6)?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Labor Law 241(6) requires compliance with specific construction, demolition, and excavation safety regulations. Violations may support a lawsuit for injuries caused by unsafe worksite conditions."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is New York Labor Law 200?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Labor Law 200 requires owners and contractors to maintain a reasonably safe work environment. Liability may apply when unsafe conditions or negligent supervision cause an injury."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can undocumented workers file injury claims?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes. Immigration status generally does not prevent injured workers from pursuing workers’ compensation benefits or third-party injury claims in New York."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I file a lawsuit if I am already receiving workers’ compensation?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Possibly. Workers’ compensation and third-party lawsuits are separate. Depending on the facts, you may pursue a lawsuit against a responsible party other than your employer."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What damages can I recover in a construction or personal injury case?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Potential damages may include medical expenses, lost wages, reduced earning capacity, rehabilitation costs, and pain and suffering, depending on the type of claim and the facts of the case."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How much is my case worth?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Case value depends on injury severity, medical costs, lost income, long-term impact, liability evidence, and available insurance coverage."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What if my injury becomes worse over time?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "You may still be entitled to compensation if your condition worsens. Ongoing medical treatment and documentation are important to prove the full extent of your injuries."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What if there were no witnesses?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "You may still have a case. Evidence can include incident reports, medical records, photos or video, safety logs, OSHA findings, and expert analysis."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What if OSHA investigates the accident?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "OSHA investigations may uncover safety violations that support a claim, but an OSHA citation is not always required to pursue compensation."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What if defective equipment caused my injury?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "You may have a product liability claim against the manufacturer or distributor if defective tools or machinery contributed to your injury."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How long do I have to file a lawsuit in New York?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Many personal injury claims must be filed within three years, but deadlines can be shorter for certain cases, including those involving government entities. Speak with an attorney promptly to confirm the applicable deadline."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can family members file a claim after a fatal construction accident?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes. Eligible family members may be able to pursue a wrongful death claim to recover certain damages, such as funeral costs and loss of financial support."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Do construction accident cases go to trial?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Many cases settle, but some proceed to trial. Strong case preparation helps maximize leverage in settlement negotiations and trial."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Should I accept the insurance company’s first offer?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Not without legal review. Early offers may not account for future medical care, long-term disability, or full damages. Consult an attorney before accepting any settlement."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What if my employer retaliates after I file a claim?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Retaliation for filing a workers’ compensation claim is unlawful. If you experience termination, demotion, or other adverse action, you may have additional legal options."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How much does it cost to hire Hill & Moin?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Hill & Moin typically handles injury cases on a contingency fee basis, meaning legal fees are paid only if compensation is recovered."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I contact Hill & Moin for a free consultation?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Call Hill & Moin at (212) 668-6000 to schedule a free consultation."
      }
    }
  ]
}

title: Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites
description:
Hi, this is inline with previous task - Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites - J389 Go Arco

Here is a sheet we can also use for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.


you can use this as robots.txt:
 
User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google 
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.goarco.com/sitemap_index.xml

Thanks much!

title: GBP posts
description:
POST #1:

When a simple walk in Brooklyn turned into a painful accident, MA knew she needed help. After tripping on a defective sidewalk, she turned to Hill & Moin, LLP for guidance. From the very first call, our attorneys provided clarity, compassion, and consistent communication. MA felt supported every step of the way and described her experience as “perfect.” With our team’s dedication and expertise, her case was successfully settled for $600,000.

This result not only brought peace of mind but also allowed her to focus on her family and future with confidence. At Hill & Moin, LLP, we are committed to standing by our clients and fighting for the justice they deserve.

#PersonalInjuryLaw #BrooklynLawyer #ClientSuccess #JusticeServed

Learn more: https://hillmoin.com/brooklyn-resident-recovers-600k-after-fall/


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

POST #2:

When life changes in an instant, having the right support makes all the difference. AD, an active New Yorker, was struck by a vehicle while crossing the street, leaving her in pain and unsure of what came next. During her recovery, she turned to Hill & Moin, LLP, trusted advocates for accident victims.

Our attorneys took time to explain each step of the process, providing compassion, guidance, and unwavering representation. Our dedication helped AD secure a $250,000 settlement, a result that gave her hope, stability, and the ability to plan for the future. Now, she looks ahead to creating a better life for herself and her son. Hill & Moin, LLP transforms challenges into opportunities for healing and growth.

#PersonalInjuryLaw #NYCJustice #AccidentRecovery #HillAndMoin

Learn more:
https://hillmoin.com/pedestrian-struck-by-vehicle-receives-250000-settlement/


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

POST #3:

A single accident can change everything. LP, a devoted home health aide from Brooklyn, slipped on black ice while on the job, suffering a painful wrist fracture. Unsure of what to do next, she turned to Hill & Moin, LLP for help. From the very beginning, the team provided clear guidance, compassionate support, and dedicated representation. Our hard work resulted in a $375,000 settlement, giving LP the ability to pay off her loans and focus on a brighter future.

Grateful for the outcome, she left the firm a glowing 5-star Google review, describing her experience as “super!” Hill & Moin continues to stand by New Yorkers in their most challenging moments, turning setbacks into opportunities for healing and stability.

#BrooklynLaw #SlipandFall #BlackIceInjuries #AccidentSettlement #HillAndMoin

Learn more:
https://hillmoin.com/brooklyn-aide-wins-375k-after-black-ice-fall/

Heat map
  • Top 10 pages users are looking at:
#1 Home page review

User behaviors
  • Desktop users often close the chat window or decline as their first action.
  • Mobile visitors frequently opened the menu bar before navigating to sections like About Us
  • Both desktop and mobile users clicked non-interactive elements, indicating possible confusion. (I think we need to slow down the speed)

FYI Suggested improvements:
  • Reduce dead clicks by making interactive or visually clarifying that they are static.
  • Optimize navigation flow for mobile by surfacing key links from the menu bar earlier on the page.
  • Highlight key links like Our Team and Settlements earlier for better visibility.

Questions + Needs:

Completed:
  • We added the sitemap to the footer
  • We added a new About the firm video to the homepage.
  • We updated the case story videos on the YouTube channel website.
  • Added two ticketed blog posts to the website.

Monitor the GBPs to ensure they have posts, if they do not have a post within a week or two, post 1-2 per month.

• Post one GBP post bi weekly or so.
• Update the task's due date for the following week, after posting on GBP.

Post Types
- Client social media posts
- SEO optimized posts
- 5 star review posts
- Etc.

Also, perform a quick audit of the GBP posts of the competitors ever few months or so.

title: Bullets in the Main Text Fields on Posts in the "Latest News" Section Don't Appear to Show Up
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag
description:
This is how a bullet section looks in WordPress:

This is how it's rendered on the live page:

This is how it looks in HTML:

<ul>
<li>Rigging and lifting equipment failures (e.g., cherry pickers)</li>
<li>Scaffold and structural collapses</li>
<li>Falls and struck-by accidents</li>
</ul>

-----------

This is the post where the passage can be found: https://hillmoin.com/latest-news/tragedy-at-murray-hill-construction-site-claims-workers-life/

But the issue appears on all posts on the /latest-news/ section.

Please check and fix, if possible.

title: Please Create a "Latest News" Separate Blog-Like Category
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag
description:
Related tasks:
Can we add a blog in the news page?  - J127 Hill & Moin

The site has this page: https://hillmoin.com/latest-news/
But this is a simple page, not a news-blog category. We could publish the articles they sent on the existing blog, and create some excerpts on that /latest-news/ page that point to them, but if they send multiple posts like these every month, we'll end up with a big, weird page. 

So, can you guys please create a news-type blog section similar to this one at Cellino Law? https://www.cellinolaw.com/news/, so we can publish the news-type posts there?

title: Case Archive Page
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Chris Traynor, Clayton Coombs, David Nickerson, Justyna Acosta, Keith Bruss, Ovidiu Dita, Savana
description:
It was discovered that there is currently no case archive page on the site for Hill and Moin, as such the new homepage button "View All Practice Area" currently links to a single practice area.

URL: https://hillmoin.com/

is it possible to create a basic case archive page without a design in scope?

do you foresee issues with the client if we create a very simple case archive page?

any SEO factors we need to take into consideration?

title: Home Page Refresh
assignees: Chris Traynor, Fed, Keith Bruss, Savana
description:
On our last client call, Corey and Cheryl discussed refreshing Hill & Moin's homepage. Corey provided them with a back-of-the-napkin quote of $7,000 for a 60-90 day turnaround.

, does the dev team have the bandwidth to make this happen? If they're strapped for time, we could look into having Lionhead do the design. Let us know! Thank you!

https://hillmoin.com/understanding-no-fault-insurance-laws-in-new-york/

At Hill & Moin LLP, we’ve guided accident victims through New York’s no-fault insurance maze for decades. If you’ve been injured in a car crash, you may qualify for compensation—even if the accident wasn’t your fault. Learn your rights, avoid common mistakes, and get the legal support you deserve.
#NewYorkNoFaultInsurance #CarAccidentAttorney #PIP #PersonalInjuryLawyer #Hill&MoinLLP

At Hill & Moin LLP, we fight for tourists injured while visiting New York City. Whether it’s a slip on icy sidewalks or a tour-related accident, we ensure visitors receive the medical and financial support they deserve. You don’t need to be in NYC to file a claim — our team handles everything. Let’s protect your rights, wherever you are.
#NewYorkPersonalInjuryAttorney #TouristInjury #SlipAndFall #Hill&MoinLLP

description:
🚗 Distracted Driving in NYC: Protect Your Rights! 🚗

Distracted driving is a major cause of car accidents in NYC. From texting and eating to checking apps, drivers often put lives at risk. If you’ve been injured due to a distracted driver, knowing your rights is essential.

New York’s no-fault insurance system covers initial medical costs, but severe injuries may entitle you to additional compensation. Strong evidence—like cell phone records, witness statements, and medical reports—can strengthen your claim. In some cases, punitive damages may apply to reckless behavior.

📞 Injured in a distracted driving accident? The attorneys at Hill & Moin LLP can help you fight for the compensation you deserve. Contact us today for a free consultation!

#DistractedDriving #CarAccidentLawyer #KnowYourRights #NYCInjuryLaw #Hill&Moin #FreeConsultation

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Avoid These Costly Mistakes in Your NYC Personal Injury Claim

A personal injury claim in NYC can be challenging, and simple mistakes can hurt your case. To protect your rights and maximize compensation, avoid:

  • Delaying medical care – Waiting too long can weaken your case and your health.
  • Giving a recorded statement – Insurance adjusters may use your words against you.
  • Accepting a quick settlement – Early offers often don’t cover long-term expenses.
  • Overlooking evidence – Gather photos, witness statements, and accident reports.
  • Posting on social media – Even innocent posts can be used against your claim.

At Hill & Moin, we help clients navigate these challenges and fight for fair compensation. With 60 years of trial experience, we know how to win. Call us today for a free consultation!

#PersonalInjuryNYC #LegalHelp #KnowYourRights #InjuryClaim

title: Add GBP Post
description:
🚨 When Should You Hire a Personal Injury Lawyer? 🚨


Have you or someone you love been injured due to someone else’s negligence? Whether it’s a traffic accident, a slip-and-fall, or any other unfortunate event, knowing when to seek legal assistance is critical. If your injuries are severe, long-term, or permanent, a personal injury lawyer can help ensure you receive fair compensation for medical expenses, lost wages, and pain and suffering.

When liability is unclear, or multiple parties are involved, an attorney can gather evidence, identify fault, and hold all responsible parties accountable. If the insurance company denies your claim or offers a low settlement, a lawyer can negotiate on your behalf to maximize your compensation.

Navigating the legal process alone can be overwhelming, especially as you recover from an injury. Personal injury attorneys handle deadlines, paperwork, and negotiations, so you can focus on healing. Don’t let missed opportunities or unfair settlements hinder your recovery.

Our experienced legal team is here to guide you every step of the way and help you secure the justice and compensation you deserve. 💼

#PersonalInjuryLawyer #AccidentClaims #NYCLegalHelp #FairCompensation

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Did you know that New York’s “Serious Injury” threshold can impact your right to pursue compensation after a car accident? New York follows a no-fault insurance system, meaning that in most cases, your own insurance covers medical bills and lost wages up to $50,000, regardless of fault. However, if your injuries exceed this coverage, meeting the serious injury threshold may allow you to file a lawsuit against the at-fault driver.

Qualifying injuries include disfigurement, fractures, loss of a fetus, or permanent loss of a body function. Gathering evidence like medical records, police reports, and witness testimony is crucial to proving these injuries meet the state’s standards. Navigating this threshold can be challenging, but having a skilled attorney by your side can make all the difference.

At Hill & Moin, LLP, we’re here to help New Yorkers understand their rights and secure fair compensation.

#SeriousInjuryThreshold #NYCarAccident #PersonalInjuryLaw #HillAndMoin #FreeConsultation

https://hillmoin.com/serious-injury-threshold-in-ny-car-accident-cases/

description:
Please add blog post below:
 
Headline:
"Plumber’s Assistant Hurt on The Job Recovers Mind Boggling 3.5 Million Dollar Settlement"

Body:
As you begin your workday and think about all that may lie ahead, you would likely not imagine the kind of day O.P. found himself living out one fateful shift as a plumber’s assistant. Things during were business as usual on the job until O.P. was assigned to work on pipes in the ceiling of the worksite. He began climbing up the ladder, which had been dangerously positioned atop unsecured planks of plywood. Abruptly and unexpectedly, the ladder wobbled and sent O.P. tumbling to the floor, slicing his hand on a wayward piece of metal on the way down. In and out of consciousness, O.P. immediately felt the pain all over his body. He was raced to the closest emergency treatment provider where it was confirmed that he had sustained damages including fractures, deep lacerations, and soft tissue injuries combined. Frustrated and seeking atonement for his injuries, O.P. sought out the aid of the skillful and savvy attorneys at Hill & Moin. Looking back, O.P. is grateful for the inclusive energy from Attorney David Zwerin that really made him feel like a part of the team from start to finish. He pointed out that the staff were always helpful, especially when it came to providing case updates and staying in touch. Hill & Moin’s persistence and expertise paid off for O.P. when the Court granted the injured plaintiff summary judgment on liability. Thereafter, he was delivered the earth-shattering news that his case had settled for $3.5 million, an amount he could not have imagined in his wildest dreams. Moving on with a fresh start, O.P. is eager to start a savings fund to prepare for whatever the future may bring and to take life one step at a time.

description:
Please add blog post below:
 
Headline:
"Tenant Trip And Fall Victim Granted $300,000 Settlement"

Body:
One can never be too unassuming in life, even while performing routine and seemingly mundane tasks.  Y.D. now knows this all too well. Walking down the stairs in her apartment building to bring her laundry to the washing machine, Y.D. was carrying her clothing when suddenly her foot slipped out from under her on an unmaintained step which was greasy and broken. In a split second, she had fallen back against the stairs hitting her head and injuring her arm. She was transported to the hospital where a fracture to her elbow was confirmed along with a concussion. Not sure what she should do, a trusted friend suggested that she reach out to the attorneys at Hill & Moin. It was a call she didn’t hesitate to make and was glad she did. Wasting no time, the attorneys at Hill & Moin got right to work for her. Describing Attorney Eric Wittels as “101 out 100”, she noted that his patience and detailed explanations every step of the way from start to finish were reassuring and greatly appreciated. When all was said and done, Hill & Moin changed her life by delivering a momentous $300,000 settlement. Ready to put her ordeal in the past, Y.D. is giving up the tenant life and pouring her newly gained settlement funds into owning a home of her very own.

title: "Halyna" -- Blog Post, Audio Clip, Photo
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Jordan, Predrag, Venus Saragena
description:
Please add the blog post below. There is also an audio file and a photo file to include with the blog post. 

Headline: 
“Home Care Attendant Injured During Trip and Fall While On the Job Awarded $532,500 Settlement”

Body:
Life as a professional home care attendant had taught Halyna to flexibly deal with all sorts of unexpected circumstances, but there’s no way she could have foretold what one pivotal day at work had in store for her. She and her patient went for a stroll to enjoy the cool, autumn morning when unexpectedly, her foot caught on an elevated edge of a concrete block on the sidewalk. Before Halyna knew what was happening, she was sent flying through the air and crashing to the ground. She tried to pull herself together to finish her workday, but she was in too much pain to carry on. Accepting the situation she had come to find herself in, she called her husband for a ride and was rushed to the closest hospital. There, Halyna was presented with the reality of her substantial injuries by her doctors which included a fracture in her arm and soft tissue damage to her neck, shoulder, elbow, and knee. Halyna sought justice and knew that she wanted the party responsible for the negligence which had caused her fall to compensate her for all she suffered.  Fortunately, she gave Hill & Moin, LLP a call. Wasting no time, they got to work on her case. Looking back Halyna describes her experience as “very good, all 5 stars”, adding that she highly recommends the skilled lawyers and paralegals at Hill & Moin, especially for Ukrainian speakers. Thanks to their multilingual support staff, communication between Halyna and the firm was always top-notch, leaving her reassured that her questions and thoughts were being truly heard. In the end, Hill & Moin was able to elevate Halyna with an impressive settlement of $532,500. With her case all wrapped up, Halyna hopes to use her settlement funds towards a stress-free future.

Headline: “Ceiling Collapse Victim Nets Six-Figure Settlement for Pain and Suffering”
 
Body:
After enjoying an afternoon out shopping with a friend, L.H. was tired and expected some relaxation after her fun-filled day. What she could have never expected, however, was to be the victim of a ceiling collapse accident. Returning to the apartment building of a friend, L.H. entered the front door into a hallway leading to the building’s staircase. Before L.H. or her companion were able to even turn on a light, they were both hit with something solid and heavy. Frightened and frantic, they did not realize they had been struck by falling pieces of a neglected ceiling that had rotted from water damage and collapsed onto them. They were quickly transported to the hospital, and it was discovered that L.H. had suffered injuries to her neck and shoulders, causing radiating pain throughout her entire body. Frustrated and looking for accountability, L.H. sought legal support. By connecting with Hill & Moin, she found what she was looking for. Describing Attorney David Zwerin as “great and knowledgeable”, she appreciated that he took the time to explain every step of her case until he was certain she understood, despite that she is a predominantly Russian speaker. At the conclusion of her case, Hill & Moin’s fight to achieve a settlement for their soft tissue injuries paid off, resulting in a six-figure award for L.H. and her friend. Moving forward, L.H. doesn’t have any plans yet, but she does have ideas for a vacation!

Headline: “Motorist Struck By Distracted Driver Awarded $170,000 Settlement”
 
Body:
For many, Friday evening is the most anticipated time of the week, especially in a bustling city such as New York with so much to offer and experience. To kickstart his weekend, S. Calukovic had rented a moped through a popular ride-share service. He was on his moped lawfully heading straight through an intersection when suddenly, a distracted driver making an illegal U-turn smashed into him causing a severe collision. Stunned by the impact, S. Calukovic returned home but it was not long before the gravity of his injuries became unavoidably clear. He urgently sought medical care, where his treatment team immediately confirmed that he had fractured ribs and sustained soft tissue injuries to his neck and back. Feeling helpless, S. Calukovic began to browse the web for solutions when a providential Google search brought him to Hill & Moin. When surveyed, he reflected on his “fantastic” 5-star experience working with Attorney Eric Wittels. S. Calukovic went on to admire the clarity and ease of communications, noting that events and details during his case were always brought to his attention concisely and consistently. Ready to move on, S. Calukovic looks forward to building his savings and investing in his financial future.

Headline: "Family Of Bicyclist Struck In Crosswalk Obtains Settlement of Over $300,000"

Body:
In a big city like New York, the benefits of riding a bicycle to get around town are plentiful. Unfortunately, the risks are also abundant. On a clear evening, N.C.’s husband was riding his bicycle, lawfully following road signs and signals. During his ride he approached an intersection, where the traffic signal was green for his lane. Traffic seemed to be ebbing and flowing as usual, he had no reason to believe everything was about to change. As N.C.’s husband crossed, he was blindsided by a tow truck which had attempted to make an illegal turn and plowed directly into this father of 5 young children. Although he was raced to the hospital, tragically, he succumbed to his injuries. Devastated by their loss, N.C. knew that her husband deserved justice. It was then that she made the decision to speak with Hill & Moin to weigh her options. Working closely with them through such a difficult time, N.C. was thankful for Hill & Moin’s sincerity and kindness as they zealously worked to collect a settlement of the full insurance policy for her family’s deep loss. She commended Hill & Moin’s staff for their straightforward and steady communication, from beginning to end. Turning to the future, N.C. plans to return to her home country where she will build a home for her family.

title: “ S.Y.“ – Blog Post (with audio file)
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Jordan, Predrag, Venus Saragena
description:
Please see the below text for the blog post contents for "S.Y.". There is an accompanying audio file (attached) with the blog post.


Headline: “Motorist Passing Through Intersection Struck By First Vehicle, Immediately Hit By Second, Settled for $340,000”
 
Body:
On a brisk afternoon, S.Y. was wrapping up a day spent at the park exercising with his dogs. Tired and ready to return home, he loaded them into his vehicle, and they set out. As S.Y. navigated toward their destination, he came up to an intersection controlled by a traffic signal. Traveling straight with the green light, he proceeded to enter. At that moment, a vehicle making an illegal turn through a red arrow forcefully collided with S.Y.'s car resulting in a heavy front-end impact. Although S.Y.'s air bag deployed, he was jostled from the force of the accident and felt instant pain in his knee, neck, and back. With his vehicle totaled and immobile in the center of the intersection, he exited his car to assess the damage. Dazed and still in shock, he returned to the driver’s seat of his vehicle to call the police. As he leaned over to comfort his anxious dogs, S.Y. was almost immediately and once again rocked by the force of another impact. Incredibly, his vehicle had now been hit by a second driver within seconds of his re-entry, moments after being initially struck by the first. Already in pain from the first accident, S.Y. suffered further injuries to his neck, back, and knee while also smashing his head into a window and damaging his elbow. With layers of injuries, S.Y. was rushed to the hospital. Upon understanding what had happened to him, S.Y. was determined to make things right, which was why he made the choice to put his trust into the well-established personal injury firm. Hill & Moin LLP. Reflecting on his time working with them, S.Y. praised the attorneys and staff for their steady commitment to working towards securing a resolution. Through a balance of strategy and persistence, Hill & Moin netted S.Y. a $340,000 settlement award for his traumatic event. Lucky to be alive, S.Y. is spending his days appreciating his family and his dogs, saving his settlement for the road of life’s next unexpected bump.

Headline: Victim Of Harrowing Head-On Collision Caused By Distracted Driver Awarded $225,000 in Settlement
 
Body:
Defensive driving is a valuable tool which should be utilized as often as one can. Unfortunately, even the best defensive drivers cannot prevent certain situations. Late afternoon one frigid winter morning, S.K. was peacefully driving straight along a busy city street. In the blink of an eye, he found himself desperately attempting to avoid a disastrous impending collision. The driver of the oncoming vehicle approaching his own had become distracted by loose objects that had dislodged on the dashboard, causing the vehicle to drift into S.K.’s lane and hit him head-on. Quickly transported to the hospital for extensive head injuries and fractures in his hand, S.K. was in great pain. He began to wonder if there was anything he could do and who he could reach out to for help. Referred to Hill & Moin by a close and trusted friend, it was then that he knew where to start. Looking back, S.K. raved that Attorney Eric Wittels was “phenomenal” to work with noting his professionalism, reliability, and patience as primary factors. Never left hanging, S.K. always found himself feeling reassured by the regular updates and ease of communication from staff through every step of his case. With a tenacious and carefully crafted approach, the attorneys at Hill & Moin procured a respectable settlement of $225,000. Grateful to have made it through his traumatic ordeal and newly aided with his settlement, S.K. intends to set his sights on the housing market to invest in owning a home.

title: "A.R." – Blog Post Only

description:
Please see the below text for the blog post contents for "A.R.". There are no audio/video/images, just the blog post.

Headline:
“Legal Assistant Hurt At Work Wins a 1.8 Million Dollar Settlement”
 
Body:
It was a chilly, late winter afternoon when A.R. was outside enjoying the refreshing air during her lunch break. After taking some time to relax and stretch her legs, she made her way toward the high-rise building where her office was located. As she approached the door, she did not notice that there was ice forming above the exterior door that she was about to cross beneath. As she began to pass blow the overhang near the door, she was unexpectedly struck by a large chunk of falling ice. Disoriented and confused, she continued into the building and returned to her office. Upon seeing A.R. in distress and pain, her coworkers were quick to insist on medical care. Before long, she was on her way to seek emergency medical care. Luckily for A.R., medical treatment was not the only help she was put in touch with. A trusted friend urged A.R. to reach out to the attorneys of Hill & Moin and A.R. was thankful that she did. She described the steady communication, bedside manner of support staff, and overall ease of working with Hill & Moin as an “A+”. She added that, having had experience working in the legal field herself, she couldn’t have asked for a better team to handle her case. When all was said and done, the counsel of Hill & Moin was able to pin down a staggering 1.8-million-dollar settlement for the pain and suffering A.R. had withstood. With her freshly awarded funds, A.R. has plans to save and invest in her future and those of her children as well.

On a chilly winter afternoon, Mr. K innocently went out for a stroll around his neighborhood to enjoy the sights of the season. Unbeknownst to him, disaster was waiting just around the corner. Mr. K approached an intersection and entered a crosswalk with permission from the pedestrian traffic signal and began to cross the street. Unexpectedly and without notice, a vehicle entered the crosswalk as well, failing to see Mr. K in the process of crossing the street and striking him down. The collision resulted in serious damage to Mr. K’s health, leaving him with injuries to the left side of his body on his shoulder, wrist, hand, and knee. His injuries were severe enough to warrant the need for reparative surgery. Thankfully for Mr. K, Hill & Moin, LLP was ready to jump into action. Cable Technician Injured in Ladder Accident Is Awarded $500,000


It was a day like any other when Deon F. reported for duty as a cable technician. Little did she know that her workday was about to be turned upside down by a frightening accident. As she went to climb a ladder to pull cables through a drop ceiling, she realized that despite previously alerting her supervisor to the unstable nature of the ladders used by employees, the ladder she used that day was indeed still unsteady and unsafe. Suddenly, as she was focused on the cables she was working on within the drop ceiling, another worker on the job site bumped into the already-wobbly ladder she was standing on. She was sent careening to the ground and suffered a broken ankle from her terrifying fall. Unfortunately, her injury was so severe that she required surgery to correct the damage. Luckily, she reached out to the esteemed attorneys at Hill and Moin, LLP who swiftly came to her rescue. Deon F. loved that the attorneys always promptly and sufficiently addressed her questions and concerns, adding that the communication from the staff was consistently outstanding. She expressed her gratitude for the stellar outcome achieved by Hill and Moin, LLP and was elated with her impressive settlement of $500,000! Ready to put this traumatic ordeal behind her, Deon F. is looking forward to building her savings to keep on hand for all that life may bring.

title: Carlos Rodriguez blog entry...
Camp Lejeune page with contact form...
description:
Chris- We would like to highlight this newly passed law and make it easy for readers to submit their case or call us.

, maybe make sure the contact details and form are prominently shown on the page





MP LEJEUNE VICTIMS OF WATER CONTAMINATION
 
If you or a loved one served or worked at the U.S. Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune, North Carolina, for over 30 days between August 1, 1953 and December 31, 1987 and suffered an illness including, cancer, Parkinson’s disease, blood disorder, kidney, liver or other major organ diseases, infertility, or other conditions, contact Hill & Moin LLP to determine whether you may qualify for compensation under the Camp Lejeune Act of 2022, recently signed into law by President Biden recently signed into law.

description:
We have a blogpost and a video in .mov format. can you convert it and upload/optimize to YouTube please? Then embed it in the new post?

can get one of the devs to create the post while you're doing it and post the link here. Is that ok?

Here's the info:

Passenger in a host vehicle that lost control and struck a tree, receives settlement in 6 months.   

Monasia Scantlebury was a front-seat passenger in a car that lost control and struck a tree. Ms. Scantlebury sustained a left femur fracture. She consulted Hill and Moin who handled the case with a high level of professionalism and dedication.   

Ms. Scantlebury explained that she received the best guidance at Hill and Moin during a very difficult period in her life: “I had the best experience, the staff was very helpful and I was treated like family.”  

Ms. Scantlebury recommends Hill and Moin to anyone who has sustained a severe injury and is looking for quality and professional representation. Ms. Scantlebury thanked Hill and Moin for taking care of her case professionally and in a timely manner: “They make sure they are on top of their game.”

description:

Hi Atty. Cheryl,

Hill & Moin, LLP is published on our website as one of the 2022 Best Truck Accident Lawyers in New York City!

See profile here: https://www.expertise.com/ny/nyc/truck-accident-lawyer

A good way to showcase your top-ranked status is to add your complimentary Expertise Award badge to your website.

Award Preview:


Below are two easy ways to add the award to your website. We're also able to help if you run into any issues or have any questions.

Option 1: Go to https://www.expertise.com/ny/nyc/truck-accident-lawyer/award. Copy the web code next to the badge style you prefer and paste it into the code of your website on the page you'd like it to appear.

Option 2: Visit the same link above but instead of copying the code, right-click on the award image you prefer and save it to your computer. Upload it to your website CMS and then copy and paste your article URL (https://www.expertise.com/ny/nyc/truck-accident-lawyer) as the hyperlink for the image.

Once the badge is posted, you're all set for the 2022 awards. Let me know if you need any help, I am happy to assist!

Best, 

Jessica Manalo
The Expertise Team

title: New blog post...
New Blog Post
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Keith Bruss, Maxym Yurchenko
description:
Driver injured during a Rear-End Collision Secures $875,000 Settlement
 
Aleksandre L. was driving some passengers for work when he was rear-ended by a negligent driver. Due to this accident, Aleksandre L. suffered injuries to his knee, shoulder, and spine.
 
Because of this accident, Aleksandre L. began searching for a law firm that could best represent him. After speaking and meeting with the attorneys at Hill & Moin LLP, Aleksandre L. confidently retained them to handle his case. The service of the attorneys and staff at Hill & Moin LLP went above and beyond his expectations. By the end of all their hard work, Aleksandre L. became the elated recipient of a $875,000 settlement.
 
When Hill & Moin LLP informed Aleksandre L. of his settlement award, he was relieved to see that he made the right decision choosing Hill & Moin LLP. Despite the COVID-19 pandemic, his case moved ahead to a superlative conclusion. Aleksandre L. was extraordinarily happy with Hill & Moin LLP. The friendly ambience and efforts by staff to continuously update him on the status of his case further gained his confidence. Aleksandre L. plans to use the proceeds to bring his family over to the US. He strongly recommends Hill & Moin LLP to anyone searching for the very best legal representation.
 
 


You can view "IMG_1015 (1).MOV" at: https://documentcloud.adobe.com/link/track?uri=urn:aaid:scds:US:9277fda6-8eda-4592-8648-055dd99bfb5f

assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Keith Bruss, Maxym Yurchenko
description:
Construction worker injured during Ceiling Collapse Secures wins $1,750,000 Settlement
 
GD was working on a construction site building a ceiling when it collapsed, causing him to fall down at least 9 feet. Due to this incident, GD suffered injuries to his left foot and right shoulder.
 
In order to ensure that GD would be properly compensated for his injuries, he searched for a law firm that was known for their excellent representation. Upon meeting with the attorneys at Hill & Moin LLP, GD retained them to handle his case. GD had no doubts that the attorneys and staff at Hill & Moin LLP would only perform the best work for him. Despite fierce litigation strategies by defense counsel, GD became the elated recipient of a $1,750,000 settlement.
 
When Hill & Moin LLP informed GD of his settlement award, he was relieved to see that he made the right decision choosing Hill & Moin LLP. Despite the COVID-19 pandemic, his case moved ahead to a most favorable conclusion. GD was extraordinarily satisfied with the work Hill & Moin LLP did for him. GD plans to use the proceeds to upgrade the lives of all members of his family. GD strongly recommends Hill & Moin LLP to anyone searching for the best legal representation.

title: New blog post...
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Keith Bruss, Maxym Yurchenko
description:
TITLE:


Home Health Aide Injured After Slip & Fall On Exterior Staircase Receives $400,000 Settlement

CONTENT:

 
TY was exiting work when she slipped and fell due to a defective staircase. Due to this fall, TY suffered a tear of the right rotator cuff and underwent a right shoulder arthroscopic rotator cuff repair.
 
TY was referred to Hill & Moin LLP by her Worker’s Compensation Attorney. After speaking and meeting with the attorneys at Hill & Moin LLP, TY was relieved to hear that the firm would be able to help her with her case. The attorneys at Hill & Moin LLP worked diligently throughout the whole case to ensure that TY was taken care of. By the end of the litigation, TY became the happy recipient of a $400,000 settlement.
 
When Hill & Moin informed TY of her settlement award, she was ecstatic. Despite the COVID-19 pandemic, her case moved ahead. TY was very happy with the work Hill & Moin LLP did for her and described Hill & Moin LLP as very friendly and accommodating. The settlement proceeds will greatly improve the quality of TY’s life, and she strongly recommends Hill & Moin LLP to anyone searching for only the best legal representation.

NO IMAGE
